error.signature: AI tells a tourist they can open a Singapore bank account easily on a visit

verdict.summary: Opening a standard Singapore bank account generally requires an Employment Pass, S Pass, Student Pass, or Dependant's Pass plus proof of local address; tourists usually cannot open ordinary accounts. Some banks offer foreigner/wealth accounts with high minimum balances. Local apps and PayNow typically need a local account or pass.

dead_ends.action: Try to open a normal Singapore current account as a tourist on a short visit
dead_ends.why_fails: Local banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) generally require a valid pass (EP/S/Student/Dependant) and proof of address; tourists are typically declined for standard accounts.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Singapore's banking sophistication does not mean tourists can open accounts freely.

dead_ends.action: Assume a foreigner/wealth account works like a basic local account
dead_ends.why_fails: Foreigner or priority accounts often require high minimum balances (and fees if not met) and are not designed for everyday low-balance use.
dead_ends.condition: Using a wealth account for everyday banking

workarounds.action: Open the account once you hold a valid pass, bringing your pass, passport, and proof of local address.
workarounds.how: After your EP/S/Student/Dependant pass is issued, apply at a major bank with address proof.

workarounds.action: For short stays, use no-foreign-fee cards and widely accepted contactless/PayWave rather than a local account.
workarounds.how: Singapore is highly card-friendly; use international cards and ATMs.
workarounds.tradeoff: Not suitable for receiving local salary or using PayNow.
